title: Rumfordia guatemalensis
taxonomy: Rumfordia guatemalensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Central America, specifically in countries such as Guatemala and Mexico. Within the family Asteraceae, Rumfordia guatemalensis is classified in the subfamily Heliantoideae. The subfamily Heliantoideae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The classification of Rumfordia guatemalensis is further divided into the tribe Heliantheae and the genus Rumfordia.
uses: Rumfordia guatemalensis is a plant species, and while there is limited information available, some studies suggest that it may have medicinal properties. parts: Rumfordia guatemalensis is known to have various medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Guatemalan folk remedy. preparations: Rumfordia guatemalensis is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
